title: Alchemical England
subtitle: From Ripley's verse to the Royal Society

narrative.intro: A late but intense alchemical culture: monastic verse, Tudor patents and prosecutions, Dee's library, Ashmole's antiquarianism, and the chymistry of Boyle and Newton.
narrative.provenance_note: Region = 'England' (77 events). Events from the ALCHEMYTIMELINEMAP corpus (largely draft-status); locations are event geocodes, not residences. Absence on this map reflects gaps in the record.
narrative.design_note: Copperplate Engraving: from Ripley's printed verse to Royal Society plates, English alchemy is a print-culture story.
